_comment: Heuristic patterns driving protocol_inconsistency_detector.py. Each detection type derives candidate inconsistencies from RFC requirement IR + code evidence, never from a hardcoded issue list. rfc_signals are deliberately broad so genuinely-mapped requirements are not filtered out before the code-evidence stage; code_signals keep a specific pattern (high signal) plus a broad fallback (catches mapped code so behavior can be reviewed downstream).

hardcoded_limit_mismatch.description: RFC requires processing all valid items (or imposes no fixed count); code stops after a hardcoded limit and returns success.

patterns.reason: loop bounded by a fixed MAX macro constant
option loop bounded by an option count rather than TLV length
hardcoded MAX/limit style macro definition
array sized by a hardcoded MAX/limit constant
function returns — potential behavior mismatch with RFC requirement

missing_required_behavior.description: RFC states a MUST/SHOULD behavior but no matching code symbol or control-flow branch implements it.

code_signals.absence: requirement_code_mapper reports trace_status=unlinked or no candidate_code_locations; for linked traces the mapped symbol must still be reviewed against the requirement

patterns.reason: function returns success without performing the required action
implementation symbol referenced — behavior should be reviewed against RFC requirement

wrong_control_flow.description: RFC requires walking the full TLV chain / extension header chain; code inspects only the first header or breaks early.

patterns.reason: early break out of header/option processing loop
control flow jumps to error path instead of continuing chain
function returns — potential behavior mismatch with RFC requirement

missing_feature_protocol_gap.description: RFC requires a protocol capability but the repository contains no implementation file/symbol for that domain.

code_signals.absence: domain code_paths yield zero indexed symbols; for linked traces the mapped symbol must still be reviewed against the required capability

patterns.reason: related implementation symbol referenced — capability coverage must be reviewed against RFC requirement

silent_drop_error_handling_mismatch.description: RFC requires handling or feedback (error/ICMP); code silently drops the packet or returns success.

patterns.reason: packet silently dropped without required ICMP/error feedback
packet freed without emitting required feedback
function returns — potential behavior mismatch with RFC requirement

timer_delay_behavior_mismatch.description: RFC requires randomized delay, suppression, or specific timer behavior; code lacks the delay/randomization.

patterns.reason: delay forced to zero where RFC requires randomized delay
no randomized delay primitive found in mapped function
function returns — potential behavior mismatch with RFC requirement

packet_path_mismatch.description: RFC requires a packet to enter the protocol stack; code bypasses, forwards, or drops it incorrectly.

patterns.reason: packet redirected to KNI/veth bypass instead of protocol stack
IPv6 packet path handling branch
function returns — potential behavior mismatch with RFC requirement
